full_prompt: Design a titanium alloy aircraft secondary structural bracket in the spirit of a canonical aircraft-bracket patent with integrated stiffening (US10131419B2-style), for mounting a generic aircraft system component to the internal fuselage frame. Envelope: 180 x 120 x 60 mm. Interfaces: fuselage-frame side has six through-holes for HI-LOK HL-12 (5/16 inch, 7.94 mm) pins on a published fastener grid (three rows of two, 40 mm longitudinal pitch, 35 mm transverse pitch per typical civil-aircraft fuselage-frame bracket practice); system-component side has a machined flange with four NAS-1351 M6 threaded holes on 80 x 50 mm pattern. The bracket carries an integrally machined stiffening rib (the claim feature) running diagonally across the main web to reduce mass while retaining buckling resistance. Material: Ti-6Al-4V per AMS 4911 (yield 795 MPa, UTS 860 MPa, Young's modulus 113.8 GPa, density 4430 kg/m^3). Load cases (values flagged as null when specific patent limit loads are not pinned): LC1 primary axial pull 15 kN along fastener grid axis (placeholder); LC2 transverse shear 8 kN across fastener grid (placeholder); LC3 combined LC1 + LC2 at 75% each; LC4 ultimate factored load = 1.5 x limit load per CS-25 (ultimate-case wording present in the patent specification). Pass/fail per CS-25.305 + EASA AMC 25.571: (R1) max von Mises at limit load (LC1-3) <= yield / 1.0 = 795 MPa with no permanent deformation (limit-load criterion); (R2) max von Mises at ultimate load (1.5 x limit) <= UTS = 860 MPa (no rupture at ultimate); binding SF 1.5 on UTS corresponds to an effective allowable of 573 MPa at limit level; (R3) linear buckling eigenvalue under worst compressive case >= 1.5 (per CS-25 buckling ultimate factor); (R4) fastener bearing stress at HI-LOK HL-12 holes <= 0.9 * yield = 715.5 MPa at limit load; (R5) mass reduction >= 20% vs a non-stiffened flat-plate baseline of equivalent envelope (the patent's central technical effect); (R6) integrated stiffening rib must be present and oriented to match the principal stress trajectory of LC3 (geometric compliance with the patent claim). Deliverables: STEP AP242 single-body part; report with per-case max stress, buckling load factor, fastener bearing check, mass-vs-baseline comparison; qualitative review that the stiffening rib orientation is consistent with the LC3 principal-stress trajectory.
short_prompt:
Design a titanium-alloy aircraft secondary-structural bracket in the spirit of a canonical aircraft-bracket patent with integrated stiffening (US10131419B2-style), mounting a generic aircraft system component to the internal fuselage frame. Central technical feature: an integrally machined stiffening rib running diagonally across the main web.

Envelope: 180 x 120 x 60 mm. Fuselage-frame side: six HI-LOK HL-12 (5/16 inch, 7.94 mm) through-holes on a 3x2 grid with 40 mm longitudinal and 35 mm transverse pitch. System-component side: four NAS-1351 M6 threaded holes on 80 x 50 mm.

Material: Ti-6Al-4V per AMS 4911.

Design per CS-25.305 + EASA AMC 25.571 with an ultimate SF of 1.5 (effective allowable 573 MPa at limit).

Load cases (magnitudes are placeholders when specific patent limits are not pinned).
- LC1 primary axial pull 15 kN along fastener grid axis.
- LC2 transverse shear 8 kN across fastener grid.
- LC3 combined LC1 + LC2 at 75% each.
- LC4 ultimate factored load = 1.5 x limit per CS-25.

Additional gates: linear buckling eigenvalue under worst compressive case >= 1.5; fastener bearing at HL-12 holes <= 0.9 * yield = 715.5 MPa at limit; mass reduction >= 20% vs a non-stiffened flat-plate baseline; rib orientation must match the LC3 principal-stress trajectory.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Verifying the stiffening-rib feature matches the patent claim is a qualitative design review step.

prompt.title: Ti-6Al-4V fuselage-frame bracket with integrated diagonal stiffening rib (civil-aircraft patent US10131419B2 style)
prompt.brief: Design a 180x120x60 mm Ti-6Al-4V bracket with an integrally machined diagonal stiffening rib following a canonical civil-aircraft US10131419B2-style concept; pass CS-25 limit/ultimate with SF 1.5.

fuselage_interface.pattern: 3 rows x 2, 40 mm longitudinal, 35 mm transverse

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: yield (no permanent deformation at limit per CS-25.305)
UTS (no rupture at 1.5x limit per CS-25.305)
pass_fail_criteria.note: Practical allowable at limit level is 860/1.5 = 573 MPa.

notes.patent_fetch: Load magnitudes (LC1, LC2) are plausible placeholders; specific US10131419B2 claim values need verification against the patent text. Setting needs_patent_fetch=true at root.
notes.numeric_source: CS-25 airframe ultimate SF 1.5; Ti-6Al-4V AMS 4911 allowables; HI-LOK HL-12 fastener data.
